item.title: INFRA: Text Editing Engine
item.intent: The core text system that handles every keystroke — must be fast enough for <16ms render and flexible enough for rich text, source view, and auto-formatting hooks.
item.description: Core text input and rendering engine. Decision needed: TextKit 2 (modern, SwiftUI-friendly, but less mature) vs UITextView with custom NSAttributedString (proven but harder to customize). Must support: text input and editing, attributed string rendering, cursor/selection management, custom text attributes for markdown styling, hooks for auto-formatting (intercept keystrokes before commit), undo manager integration, IME support for CJK input, RTL text layout, Dynamic Type font scaling. Performance: <16ms keystroke-to-render (D-PERF-2), 120fps scroll (D-PERF-3).
acceptanceCriteria: User types and sees characters appear with <16ms latency (measured via instruments)
Scrolling a 5,000-line document maintains 60fps on all devices, 120fps on ProMotion
CJK IME composition works correctly (partial input displayed, committed on confirm)
RTL text (Arabic, Hebrew) displays and edits with correct cursor behavior
Dynamic Type at all sizes (including accessibility sizes) renders without clipping or overlap
Undo manager tracks all text changes with unlimited depth
